'''Jamie Foster''' (born 27 July 1990)<ref>{{cite news|url = http://www1.skysports.com/news/12040/8598958|title =Recentlly Wigan sign Jamie after his release from Bradford|work = [[Sky Sports]]|date = 28 March 2013|quote = The 22-year-old, signed for the Bulls from his home town club St Helens in the off-season}}</ref> is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]] in 2018. His [[Rugby league positions|position]] of preference is in the {{rlp|ce}}s, but he has been known to convert to the {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|fb}}.

Foster was also a recognised [[Playing rugby league#Point scoring|goalkicker]]. He signed from local amateur club Blackbrook Royals ARLFC. As of June 2014, Jamie lives in Newton-Le-Willows, not far from where he grew up in Haydock, St. Helens. He has played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

==St Helens RLFC==
In the fourth round of the [[2010 Challenge Cup]], he finally made his first-grade début, after three years in reserve-grade rugby, playing on the wing in place of the injured [[Francis Meli]], where he kicked eight goals from as many attempts in a 56–16 win over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref name="Saints Ease Into Fifth Round Draw">{{cite news|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2440|title=Saints Ease into Fifth Round Draw|newspaper=St.helens R.f.c.|publisher=sthelensrlfc.com|date=17 April 2010|accessdate=18 April 2010|author1=Cultivate}}</ref> In his Super League début the week later, again deputising for Meli, Foster looked impressive once more with the boot scoring eight from eight in a 41–20 success over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref name="Saints up to second">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3217306,00.html |title=Saints up to second |publisher=skysports.com |date=24 April 2010 |accessdate=25 April 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100427024628/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0%2C19936%2C11070_3217306%2C00.html |archivedate=27 April 2010 }}</ref><ref name="Official Match Report">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217306|title=Official Match Report|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=24 April 2010|accessdate=25 April 2010|archive-date=1 March 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170301005448/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217306|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Saints Brush Aside Champions">{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/news/2462|title=Saints Brush Aside Champions|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=24 April 2010|accessdate=25 April 2010}}</ref> His first failure with the boot only came in his third appearance, when, in a 54–0 [[Magic Weekend]] win over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], he kicked only two from six, whilst also scoring his maiden try for the club.<ref name="St Helens 54-0 Hull KR">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3274222|title=St Helens 54–0 Hull KR|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2 May 2010|accessdate=2 May 2010|archive-date=3 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303201514/http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3274222|url-status=dead}}</ref> He missed the cut for the [[Challenge Cup]] game against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], but thanks to a recurrence of Meli's injury, Foster returned for the 34–42 loss at [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], in which he was brought off the field after ten minutes after a poor display defensively.<ref name="Saints 34-42 Salford City Reds">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217312|title=Saints 34–42 Salford City Reds|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=15 May 2010|accessdate=22 May 2010|archive-date=8 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170808153549/http://www.superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217312|url-status=dead}}</ref>

However, this did not end his run in the first team, as he started on the wing against Rovers in a 68–12 win,<ref name="Saints 68-12 Hull KR">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217317|title=Saints 68–12 Hull KR|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=21 May 2010|accessdate=22 May 2010|archive-date=8 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170808153812/http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3217317|url-status=dead}}</ref> before being dropped for the loss at Hull FC. However, he returned to play on the right wing in a 24–22 win at home to Huddersfield, where he kicked five goals.<ref name="Youngsters Come Of Age In Giants Win">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2591|title=Youngsters Come of Age in Giants Win|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=11 June 2010|accessdate=12 June 2010}}</ref> The next week (in round 18) he was again on the wing for a 24–26 win over rivals [[Wigan Warriors]], where he kicked three from five and scored a try.<ref name="Wonderful Saints Secure Fine Derby Victory">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2633|title=Wonderful Saints Secure Fine Derby Victory|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=20 June 2010|accessdate=20 June 2010}}</ref> He missed three games with a calf knock but returned for Saints in a centre role against Wakefield that saw him kick seven goals from nine attempts.<ref name="Saints Tame Cats In Nine-Try Romp">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/news/2701|title=Saints Tame Cats in Nine-Try Romp|publisher=saintsrlfc.com|date=25 July 2010|accessdate=26 July 2010}}</ref> He was also part of beaten finalists [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] where [[Wigan Warriors]] won 22–10 in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]].

At the start of the 2011 season, Foster scored 14 tries and 77 goals, a total of 210 points, in 13 appearances and three from the bench. His partnership with [[Francis Meli]] in the 2011 season was productive. In the first game of the 2011's [[Super League XVI]] [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] played [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in a repeat of the Super League final. In this game Saints were losing 16–0 at halftime and came back to draw 16–16 with Foster coming off the bench to kick two goals. Foster then kicked 8/10 against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in round 2. When Saints played [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], in Round 6, he scored two tries, including a round the back trick to score in the corner, and kicked five goals. In Round 12, he kicked a touchline goal to win the game for Saints against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. In the Challenge Cup Fifth Round Foster kicked 11 out of 12 goals in a 70–0 thrashing of [[Featherstone Rovers]].

Foster played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against Leeds at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|accessdate=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=21 November 2019}}</ref>
[[Royce Simmons]], the Saints coach, has said "Jamie Foster's ability to kick points is vital to the Saints. The way Foster can turn four points into six, he can break the heart of another side through that".

==Loan at Hull FC==

Near the end of the [[Super League XVII|2012 Super League]] season, Foster spent a month on loan at [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. His first game for the Black and Whites was against arch-rivals Hull Kingston Rovers, he helped his new side to a 32–18 win by kicking six goals. The week after he played in the 36–24 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] where he kicked 100% of his goals with seven goals from seven attempts. He was also part of the team which lost 48–10 to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], Foster managed to get himself a goal in this game. Foster was on form the next week against the [[Catalans Dragons]] where he recorded his first try for Hull F.C. and also kicked five goals leading his team to a 30–10 win. However, this victory was short lived as the Black & Whites were soundly beaten 42–16 by newly promoted [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], Foster got himself on the scoreboard with two goals. He was a key member in [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]'s record breaking 70–6 victory over his future club the [[Bradford Bulls]], he helped his team to this huge win by contributing two tries and nine goals which meant Hull were secure in the playoffs. His last regular season game for Hull was in a 36–10 win over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], Foster put in a fine display and kicked all six goals.

==Bradford Bulls==

Foster signed for [[Super League]] side [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] on a one-year deal.

'''2013''' – [[2013 Bradford Bulls season|2013 Season]]

Foster featured at fullback for the pre-season friendlies against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and Leeds. He featured in five consecutive games from Round 1 against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] to Round 5 against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. He was injured for Round 6. Foster featured in four consecutive games from Round 7 Hull Kingston Rovers to Round 10 against Salford. Foster was injured for Round 11–15 but returned for Round 16 Huddersfield. Foster missed Round 17–19 due to injury. He returned in Round 20 [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] to Round 27 Huddersfield. Foster featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against the [[Rochdale Hornets]]. He re-signed with the Bradford club for two years midway through the season.

'''2014''' – [[2014 Bradford Bulls season|2014 Season]]

Foster featured in the pre-season games against Hull FC, Dewsbury and Castleford. He featured in Round 1 against Castleford to Round 5 against Huddersfield. Foster missed Rounds 6–7 due to injury. He featured in Round 8 against Salford then in Round 11 Warrington to Round 14 [[Catalans Dragons]]. He was injured for Round 15–19. Foster played in Round 20 [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] to Round 21 Huddersfield then in Round 24 Hull F.C. His final game for Bradford was in Round 27 against the [[London Broncos]]. Foster featured in Round 4 [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] to Round 5 Catalans Dragons in the [[Challenge Cup]].
